text:
There once was a small town named Everglow. Everglow was a beautiful place where birds sang all day long and sunrays seemed never-ending. Everyone knew everyone else's business, though maybe not their favorite color. But there was one peculiar thing about Everglow—the old owl clock.

Every evening, the old owl atop Old Wilson's statue would wink goodbye. Now, everyone loved that. They loved owls! But they loved owl winks even more. One particularly quiet October evening, old owl winked just a tad too long. People giggled. Old Wilson smirked wider.

News spread.Children brought owlsseggs. Adults made owl pillowforts. Owl-themed cafes opened. Everglow loved owl Week!

And so did you.

*You loved owl week.*

That's my story.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
